messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Webinar: Advances in Gradient Boosting: the Power of Post-Processing. TOMORROW, 10-11 a.m., PST
messages.subject_clean: Webinar: Advances in Gradient Boosting: the Power of Post-Processing. TOMORROW, 10-11 a.m., PST
messages.body_plain:
Webinar: Advances in Gradient Boosting: the Power of Post-Processing
TOMORROW: December 14, 10-11 a.m., PST

Webinar Registration: http://2.salford-systems.com/gradientboosting-and-post-processing/ 
 
Course Outline: 
I. Gradient Boosting and Post-Processing: 
o What is missing from Gradient Boosting? 
o Why post-processing techniques are used? 

II. Applications Benefiting from Post-Processing: Examples from a variety of industries. 
o Financial Services 
o Biomedical 
o Environmental 
o Manufacturing 
o Adserving 

III. Typical Post-Processing Steps 

IV. Techniques: 
o Generalized Path Seeker (GPS): modern high-speed LASSO-style regularized regression. 
o Importance Sampled Learning Ensembles (ISLE): identify and reweight the most influential trees. 
o Rulefit: ISLE on ?steroids.? Identify the most influential nodes and rules. 

V. Case Study Example: 
o Output/Results without Post-Processing 
o Output/Results with Post-Processing 
o Demo

Webinar: Advances in Gradient Boosting: the Power of Post-Processing
TOMORROW: December 14, 10-11 a.m., PST

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Optmatch Package Question
messages.body_plain:
Hello ,?

My optmatch package is loaded on R and otherwise running fine.?
I get an error after lcds successfully completes a logistic regression and?
I then try to obtain a propensity score:?

pdist <- pscore.dist(lcds)?
Error: could not find function "pscore.dist"?

Since pscore.dist is part of the optmatch package,?
does anyone know why this command would fail to run
pscore.dist ??
I searched the help files, other online sources, could find no answer for?
this. Any advice would be greatly appreciated!?

Thank you ,?
Michael Adolph MD MPH?
Cancer Pain & Palliative Medicine Service?
Ohio State University?
Columbus, Ohio, USA
